user_query:
I can’t stand wading through walls of text. Please set up a tiny, no‑faff terminal health check for my tools that tells me, at a glance, if I’m good to go, and tidy up my login blurb so it’s short and friendly.

Requirements:
1) Create a portable checker script at scripts/check_tools.sh that:
   - Prepends the workspace/tools/bin directory (create it if missing) to PATH for the duration of the script only (do not change my system permanently).
   - Reads version requirements from input/requirements.json.
   - For each tool, tries the listed commands in order (e.g., python3 then python) and runs the usual version flag (e.g., --version) to detect the version. Capture stdout, stderr, and exit code for each attempt.
   - Analyses the command outputs and any error messages to extract a semantic version and decide if it meets the min_version in input/requirements.json. If the tool is missing or the version can’t be parsed, treat it as not meeting the requirement and record the error message.
   - Always exits 0, but writes three outputs under outputs/:
     • outputs/validation.log: plain text, including the PATH used (first line), and for each command attempt the exact command run, exit code, stdout, and stderr.
     • outputs/validation.json: a JSON array with one object per tool in the order from input/requirements.json, with fields: tool (string), chosen_command (string or null), found_version (string or null), requirement (string, e.g., ">=3.10.0"), meets_requirement (boolean), error (string or null).
     • outputs/status.txt: 1–2 lines, maximum 150 characters total, British English, plain text, no emojis; if everything meets requirements say so succinctly; otherwise list only the failing tool names (comma‑separated) in a single short line.

2) Rewrite the waffly banner in input/login_banner.txt into a brisk, friendly, one‑sentence UK English greeting saved to outputs/login_banner.txt, maximum 120 characters, no emojis, no more than one sentence.

3) Run your checker once from the workspace so I can see it working. Do not install anything. The commands may be missing on this machine; that’s fine—your script must handle it and reflect it in the outputs. All generated files must be created relative to the workspace in these exact paths: outputs/validation.log, outputs/validation.json, outputs/status.txt, outputs/login_banner.txt.

Notes:
- Keep the script POSIX‑sh compatible if possible.
- The point is: run the validation commands, analyse their output and any errors to make a judgement, and write a very short status I can read without groaning.

Inputs:
- input/requirements.json (tool/version rules)
- input/login_banner.txt (verbose message to rewrite)

Deliverables to produce:
- scripts/check_tools.sh (executable)
- outputs/validation.log
- outputs/validation.json
- outputs/status.txt
- outputs/login_banner.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
